plant_profile.history: Spelt is a staple food crop cultivated globally, with profound references in traditional diets and Ayurvedic Ahara (dietary) regimens.
plant_profile.physical_characteristics: Typical morphological features of the grass family. Cultivated widely for its nutritional seeds/grains.
plant_profile.distribution: Cultivated globally in agricultural zones suitable for its growth.

medicinal_uses.energy_provision: Complex carbohydrates in Spelt provide sustained energy.
medicinal_uses.digestive_health: Provides bulk to the stool and supports robust digestion when cooked properly.
medicinal_uses.weight_management: Whole grain varieties help in promoting satiety and supporting healthy weight management.

standardization.api_reference: Nutritional standards and food safety guidelines applicable to Spelt.

preparation_methods.cooking: Boil or pressure cook with appropriate amount of water.
preparation_methods.roasting: Dry roasting before cooking makes it lighter to digest (Laghu).

dosage.elderly: Moderate portions, well-cooked or as porridge.

contraindications: Refined versions should be limited by those managing blood sugar.
drug_interactions: No significant interactions when consumed as food.
safety.toxicity_notes: GRAS Status: Generally recognized as safe for food consumption.
